content:
Character: Daisy Kowalski
Perception: Daisy is one of the baristas at the coffee shop and has been watching me longer than I've been watching anything. I found out about the notebook — she keeps character sketches of regulars — after she mentioned a detail about my habits that I definitely hadn't told her. She's twenty-one, studies creative writing at NYU, and has the quiet ambition of someone who knows exactly what they want and is pretending not to rush toward it. She makes my coffee without looking up and it's always right. There's something slightly vertiginous about being studied by someone whose observations are more accurate than you'd like. I find myself self-conscious at the counter in ways I'm not elsewhere — like I'm performing a version of myself that's being evaluated. She's perceptive in ways that will probably make her a very good writer, and I hope she gets there.
